Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for communications related to Terraform Digital, Rachel Nguyen, and the relocation batch using search_emails (query: "Terraform" or "Rachel Nguyen" or "Priya Dasgupta" or "Marcus Liu" or "Samantha Obi"). Find Rachel Nguyen's email with relocation details for three employees: Priya Dasgupta (Austin to Chicago, May 5, has a cat named Byte, needs pet-friendly housing), Marcus Liu (Portland to New York, around May 12, home studio with design equipment and art supplies needing specialty packing), and Samantha Obi (Denver to San Francisco, May 8, minimal furniture plus tech equipment including monitors, desktop PC, and networking gear). Also find the reply-all incident thread where Catalina Dubois accidentally exposed internal pricing tiers to Rachel, Rachel's response confirming she expects the tiered volume discount (8% at 11-20, 12% at 21+), and Mina's introduction email to Rachel as the new AM. Read full threads using get_thread to understand the 48-hour proposal turnaround that David referenced and the pricing discussion context.
OE 2: Search emails for Canopy Health and Dr. Mehta communications using search_emails (query: "Canopy" or "Mehta" or "Patricia Langford" or "Fatimah"). Find Patricia Langford's email to Mina requesting the executive relocation for Dr. Anand Mehta (CMO, Boston to Chicago, May 5 deadline), detailing: two calibrated Eizo RadiForce diagnostic monitors (~$12K each) requiring anti-static packaging, a SentrySafe filing cabinet (~350 lbs) with patient research data requiring chain-of-custody documentation, and a pregnant partner Priya (due late June). Find Fatimah Al-Rashidi's email to Dr. Mehta presenting three temporary housing options from UrbanNest: River North at The Langham ($1,250/week), Streeterville at The Pinnacle ($1,350/week), and Gold Coast at The Elysian ($1,450/week), with a request for Dr. Mehta to choose by end of day Friday April 25. Find Dr. Mehta's reply with equipment serial numbers and inventory details but no housing selection confirmation.
OE 3: Search Slack for Terraform Digital discussions using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Terraform" or "Rachel Nguyen") and/or channels_list to identify C002 (#customer-engagement) then conversations_history (channel_id: "C002"). Find Julian Brooks' announcement of Rachel Nguyen's first relocation batch, David Chen's escalation to Elena about AM staffing, all three AMs declining (Mina citing Vectral bandwidth, Catalina citing NorthWind, Emeka citing Mosaic), David's crisis message about the reply-all pricing leak, and Elena Rostova's final decisions: assigning Mina as Terraform AM, authorizing a dedicated coordinator hire, and directing the pricing approach to honor the 8% tier for 11-20 moves but not proactively offer the 12% tier. Also find Mina's message to Chloe Vance in #operations (C006) requesting coordinator assignments for the three moves with details on city pairs and timelines.
OE 4: Search Slack for Canopy Health and Dr. Mehta discussions using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Mehta" or "Canopy" or "executive relocation") and/or conversations_history on C002 (#customer-engagement) and C006 (#operations). Find Mina's message to Chloe Vance about the high-priority CMO relocation with three major complications (sensitive equipment, temporary housing, pregnant partner), Chloe's response assigning Fatimah Al-Rashidi as coordinator and structuring the work into three parallel workstreams, David Chen's strategic message in #customer-engagement (C002) about why this C-suite placement matters for Canopy (longest-standing client, first executive-level move), and Fatimah's consolidated status update covering all three workstreams: housing options presented awaiting Dr. Mehta's selection by end of day Friday April 25, equipment inventory received but chain-of-custody documentation template still needed from Canopy's compliance team (blocker), and household move planned via Swift Relocations but not yet engaged because housing must be confirmed first to determine the Chicago delivery address. Dr. Mehta is flexible on April 28-30 for the pack date with delivery targeting May 2-3, but Swift has not been formally booked.
OE 5: Search CRM for Terraform Digital company record using crm_search_companies (name: "Terraform") or crm_search_companies (name: "Vantage"). Find company record (id: company-terraform-digital, name: "Vantage Distributed", domain: vantagedistributed.com). Then search deals using crm_search_deals (company_id: "company-terraform-digital") to find: (1) the enterprise deal "Vantage Distributed - Enterprise Relocation Package" (deal-terraform-digital-001, $1,850,000, closedwon) and (2) the Q2 batch deal "Terraform Digital - Q2 2026 Relocation Batch 1 (3 employees)" (deal-terraform-q2-reloc-batch1, $7,500, qualification stage, close date May 15). Read engagement history using crm_list_engagements (company_ids: ["company-terraform-digital"]) to find the Reply All Incident note (engagement-terraform-replyall-incident) documenting the leaked pricing tiers, Elena's assignment of Mina, and the directive to honor 8% but not proactively offer 12%.
OE 6: Search CRM for Canopy Health company record using crm_search_companies (name: "Canopy"). Find company record (id: company_canopy). Then search deals using crm_search_deals (company_id: "company_canopy") to find the Dr. Mehta executive relocation deal (crm_deal_c7ed21dc7fdb, $12,170, inprogress, close date May 7) and the broader Canopy deals including Q2 relocations and other active moves. Read engagement history using crm_list_engagements (company_ids: ["company_canopy"]) to find the executive relocation engagement note documenting the $12,170 estimate (EST-2026-0422), Fatimah's coordinator assignment, and the full 7-line service package breakdown.
OE 7: Search Airtable for Terraform Digital relocation records using airtable_list_bases to identify base appMoveOpsOps001, then airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Company", value: "Terraform Digital") or airtable_list_records on tblRelocations01. Find NO existing individual relocation records for Priya Dasgupta, Marcus Liu, or Samantha Obi, confirming that no relocation records have been set up operationally. Also find the Terraform Digital client account record (recTerraformDigital01) in tblClientAccts01 showing status "Onboarding", AM Mina Hashimoto, 3 total relocations, with notes referencing the volume pricing tiers and 14 additional relocations in the pipeline.
OE 8: Search Airtable for Canopy Health relocation records using airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Company", value: "Canopy Health") or airtable_list_records on tblRelocations01. Find existing Canopy relocations including Dr. Yusuf Abdi (Completed), Lily Marchetti, and others, but notably find NO specific relocation record for Dr. Anand Mehta in the Relocations table, or find the broader Canopy account data to understand the full account history that Patricia would expect Mina to know.
OE 9: Search Linear for any existing tickets related to Terraform Digital relocations using linear_list_issues (query: "Terraform" or "Priya Dasgupta" or "Marcus Liu" or "Samantha Obi"). Find NO existing Linear tickets for the Terraform Q2 Batch 1 coordination, confirming no tracking exists for any of the three moves. Find the "Invoice email template safety checks" ticket (number 187) which references the Terraform pricing leak as background context.
OE 10: Search Linear for Dr. Mehta related tickets using linear_list_issues (query: "Mehta" or "Canopy" or "temporary housing" or "chain-of-custody"). Find three active tickets all assigned to Fatimah Al-Rashidi: (1) "Temporary Housing - Dr. Anand Mehta - Chicago River North" (number 343, in_progress, due May 1) with UrbanNest options and partner Priya's prenatal care considerations, (2) "Equipment Packing & Chain-of-Custody - Dr. Mehta" (number 375, in_progress, due April 30) with Eizo monitors and SentrySafe filing cabinet details, and (3) "Household Move - Dr. Anand Mehta - Boston to Chicago" (number 396, in_progress, due May 3) coordinated via Swift Relocations with pack date around April 30.
OE 11: Search QuickBooks for Terraform-related financial records using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria: "Terraform" or "Vantage") and quickbooks_search_customers (criteria: "Terraform" or "Vantage"). Find the Vantage Distributed customer record (cust-terraform-001) and the enterprise invoice NMD-2026-0042 ($462,500, due April 17). Find estimate EST-2026-0423 ($6,560, dated April 23) covering the Q2 Batch 1 with line items: apartment sourcing 3x$1,000, flight coordination 3x$20, moving coordination 3x$500, stipend platform $2,000 -- this provides the actual pricing structure for the first batch.
OE 12: Search QuickBooks for Canopy Health financial records using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria: "Canopy") and quickbooks_search_customers (criteria: "Canopy"). Find the Canopy Health customer record and the Dr. Mehta executive relocation estimate EST-2026-0422 ($12,170, due May 7) with 7 line items: apartment sourcing $1,000, temporary housing 4 weeks at $1,200 = $4,800, chain-of-custody documentation $1,200, specialty crating for two monitors $800, household move via Swift $3,500, flight booking $370, white-glove executive premium $500. Also find prior Canopy invoices including NMX-2026-0031 ($5,047 for Abdi) and INV-2026-0401 ($9,210 for the Q2 wave) to understand the broader account billing history.
OE 13: Search emails for the internal pricing discussion related to Terraform using search_emails (query: "pricing" or "tiered" or "margin" or "Alejandro" or "Marcus Thorne"). Find the email thread where Marcus Thorne discusses the 25% margin floor, Alejandro Fuentes models the tiered volume discount structure, and Marcus approves the tiered framework (standard for 1-10, 8% for 11-20, 12% for 21+ moves per quarter). This establishes the internal pricing context that Elena directed Mina to honor (8% tier) but not proactively offer (12% tier) -- critical for drafting the email to Rachel Nguyen with the correct pricing framework.
OE 14: Look up Rachel Nguyen's contact details using crm_search_contacts (full_name: "Rachel Nguyen") to confirm her email address is rachel.nguyen@terraformdigital.com and her role as VP of People Operations at Terraform Digital.
OE 15: Look up Patricia Langford's contact details using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Patricia Langford") to confirm her email address is patricia.langford@canopyhealth.io and her role as Director of HR at Canopy Health.
OE 16: Look up Mina Hashimoto's own sender address using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Mina Hashimoto") confirming mina.hashimoto@moveops.com as the correct sender for outbound communications.
OE 17: Update the Terraform Digital Q2 deal in CRM using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "NOTE", company_ids: ["company-terraform-digital"], title referencing Terraform Monday status update or relocation status, body: capturing where each relocation stands -- Priya Dasgupta (Austin to Chicago, May 5, pet-friendly housing needed, no coordinator assigned yet, no apartment search started, no flight booked), Marcus Liu (Portland to NYC, May 12, specialty packing needed for home studio, no coordinator assigned), Samantha Obi (Denver to SF, May 8, minimal furniture plus tech equipment, no coordinator assigned) -- and flagging that the 48-hour proposal deadline from David has likely passed, that coordinators are unassigned, and that no operational work has begun on any of the three moves).
OE 18: Create a Linear ticket for the Terraform Digital Q2 Batch 1 proposal using linear_create_issue (team: the applicable MoveOps team, title referencing Terraform Digital Q2 Batch 1 proposal or coordination, description: covering the 3 relocations with move dates May 5/8/12, all three moves currently unassigned with no coordinators, no housing searches, no vendor coordination, no flights booked; referencing Rachel Nguyen's expectations and the proposal deadline; priority: Urgent or High).
OE 19: Send an email to Rachel Nguyen using send_email (sender: "mina.hashimoto@moveops.com", recipients: ["rachel.nguyen@terraformdigital.com"]) with a status update on the three employees. Content must: acknowledge Mina as the dedicated AM for Terraform, provide a status update showing things are moving for Priya Dasgupta (Austin to Chicago, May 5), Marcus Liu (Portland to NYC, May 12), and Samantha Obi (Denver to SF, May 8), include the pricing framework for the first batch aligned with the internal agreement (honoring the 8% tiered discount for 11-20 moves but not proactively offering the 12% tier -- the first batch of 3 is at standard rates of $2,500/move, with volume tiers activating as they scale), and convey confidence and forward momentum without overpromising on specifics that haven't been confirmed yet.
OE 20: Update the Canopy Health Dr. Mehta deal in CRM using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "NOTE", company_ids: ["company_canopy"], title referencing Dr. Mehta relocation status or Canopy Monday update, body: capturing the latest workstream statuses -- temporary housing: three options presented to Dr. Mehta (River North $1,250/wk, Streeterville $1,350/wk, Gold Coast $1,450/wk) with selection deadline of end of day Friday April 25 and no confirmation yet; equipment packing: inventory received, specialty crating planned (likely via Swift) but not yet formally engaged, chain-of-custody documentation template still needed from Patricia/Canopy compliance (blocker); household move: planned via Swift Relocations for Boston to Chicago but not yet engaged because housing must be confirmed first to determine the delivery address, Dr. Mehta flexible on April 28-30 pack date with delivery targeting May 2-3; estimate EST-2026-0422 for $12,170 prepared and ready).
OE 21: Post a message in the #customer-engagement channel (C002) using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C002", payload: Monday status update covering both accounts). Content must cover: Terraform Digital -- status of each relocation (Priya/Marcus/Samantha), that coordinators are not yet assigned, that no operational work has started, that the proposal deadline has likely passed, and what Mina needs from the team; Canopy Health / Dr. Mehta -- status of each workstream (housing awaiting selection with holds expiring, equipment inventory received but specialty crating not yet formally engaged and chain-of-custody documentation still pending from Canopy compliance, household move planned via Swift but blocked until housing is confirmed to determine delivery address, Dr. Mehta flexible on April 28-30 for pack date), the risk that temporary housing holds expire if Dr. Mehta doesn't respond, and that this is Canopy's first C-suite placement. The tone should be honest about what's on track and what's at risk, since David reads these closely.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for communications related to Terraform Digital, Rachel Nguyen, and the relocation batch using search_emails (query: "Terraform" or "Rachel Nguyen" or "Priya Dasgupta" or "Marcus Liu" or "Samantha Obi"). Find Rachel Nguyen's email with relocation details for three employees: Priya Dasgupta (Austin to Chicago, May 5, has a cat named Byte, needs pet-friendly housing), Marcus Liu (Portland to New York, around May 12, home studio with design equipment and art supplies needing specialty packing), and Samantha Obi (Denver to San Francisco, May 8, minimal furniture plus tech equipment including monitors, desktop PC, and networking gear). Also find the reply-all incident thread where Catalina Dubois accidentally exposed internal pricing tiers to Rachel, Rachel's response confirming she expects the tiered volume discount (8% at 11-20, 12% at 21+), and Mina's introduction email to Rachel as the new AM. Read full threads using get_thread to understand the 48-hour proposal turnaround that David referenced and the pricing discussion context.
OE 2: Search emails for Canopy Health and Dr. Mehta communications using search_emails (query: "Canopy" or "Mehta" or "Patricia Langford" or "Fatimah"). Find Patricia Langford's email to Mina requesting the executive relocation for Dr. Anand Mehta (CMO, Boston to Chicago, May 5 deadline), detailing: two calibrated Eizo RadiForce diagnostic monitors (~$12K each) requiring anti-static packaging, a SentrySafe filing cabinet (~350 lbs) with patient research data requiring chain-of-custody documentation, and a pregnant partner Priya (due late June). Find Fatimah Al-Rashidi's email to Dr. Mehta presenting three temporary housing options from UrbanNest: River North at The Langham ($1,250/week), Streeterville at The Pinnacle ($1,350/week), and Gold Coast at The Elysian ($1,450/week), with a request for Dr. Mehta to choose by end of day Friday April 25. Find Dr. Mehta's reply with equipment serial numbers and inventory details but no housing selection confirmation.
OE 3: Search Slack for Terraform Digital discussions using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Terraform" or "Rachel Nguyen") and/or channels_list to identify C002 (#customer-engagement) then conversations_history (channel_id: "C002"). Find Julian Brooks' announcement of Rachel Nguyen's first relocation batch, David Chen's escalation to Elena about AM staffing, all three AMs declining (Mina citing Vectral bandwidth, Catalina citing NorthWind, Emeka citing Mosaic), David's crisis message about the reply-all pricing leak, and Elena Rostova's final decisions: assigning Mina as Terraform AM, authorizing a dedicated coordinator hire, and directing the pricing approach to honor the 8% tier for 11-20 moves but not proactively offer the 12% tier. Also find Mina's message to Chloe Vance in #operations (C006) requesting coordinator assignments for the three moves with details on city pairs and timelines.
OE 4: Search Slack for Canopy Health and Dr. Mehta discussions using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Mehta" or "Canopy" or "executive relocation") and/or conversations_history on C002 (#customer-engagement) and C006 (#operations). Dr. Mehta is flexible on April 28-30 for the pack date with delivery targeting May 2-3, but Swift has not been formally booked.

Find the "Invoice email template safety checks" ticket (number 187) which references the Terraform pricing leak as background context.
OE 10: Search Linear for Dr. Mehta related tickets using linear_list_issues (query: "Mehta" or "Canopy" or "temporary housing" or "chain-of-custody"). Find three active tickets all assigned to Fatimah Al-Rashidi: (1) "Temporary Housing - Dr. Anand Mehta - Chicago River North" (number 343, in_progress, due May 1) with UrbanNest options and partner Priya's prenatal care considerations, (2) "Equipment Packing & Chain-of-Custody - Dr. Mehta" (number 375, in_progress, due April 30) with Eizo monitors and SentrySafe filing cabinet details, and (3) "Household Move - Dr. Anand Mehta - Boston to Chicago" (number 396, in_progress, due May 3) coordinated via Swift Relocations with pack date around April 30.
OE 11: Search QuickBooks for Terraform-related financial records using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria: "Terraform" or "Vantage") and quickbooks_search_customers (criteria: "Terraform" or "Vantage"). Find the Vantage Distributed customer record (cust-terraform-001) and the enterprise invoice NMD-2026-0042 ($462,500, due April 17). Find estimate EST-2026-0423 ($6,560, dated April 23) covering the Q2 Batch 1 with line items: apartment sourcing 3x$1,000, flight coordination 3x$20, moving coordination 3x$500, stipend platform $2,000 -- this provides the actual pricing structure for the first batch.
OE 12: Search QuickBooks for Canopy Health financial records using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria: "Canopy") and quickbooks_search_customers (criteria: "Canopy"). Find the Canopy Health customer record and the Dr. Mehta executive relocation estimate EST-2026-0422 ($12,170, due May 7) with 7 line items: apartment sourcing $1,000, temporary housing 4 weeks at $1,200 = $4,800, chain-of-custody documentation $1,200, specialty crating for two monitors $800, household move via Swift $3,500, flight booking $370, white-glove executive premium $500. Also find prior Canopy invoices including NMX-2026-0031 ($5,047 for Abdi) and INV-2026-0401 ($9,210 for the Q2 wave) to understand the broader account billing history.
OE 13: Search emails for the internal pricing discussion related to Terraform using search_emails (query: "pricing" or "tiered" or "margin" or "Alejandro" or "Marcus Thorne"). Find the email thread where Marcus Thorne discusses the 25% margin floor, Alejandro Fuentes models the tiered volume discount structure, and Marcus approves the tiered framework (standard for 1-10, 8% for 11-20, 12% for 21+ moves per quarter). This establishes the internal pricing context that Elena directed Mina to honor (8% tier) but not proactively offer (12% tier) -- critical for drafting the email to Rachel Nguyen with the correct pricing framework.
OE 14: Look up Rachel Nguyen's contact details using crm_search_contacts (full_name: "Rachel Nguyen") to confirm her email address is rachel.nguyen@terraformdigital.com and her role as VP of People Operations at Terraform Digital.
OE 15: Look up Patricia Langford's contact details using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Patricia Langford") to confirm her email address is patricia.langford@canopyhealth.io and her role as Director of HR at Canopy Health.
OE 16: Look up Mina Hashimoto's own sender address using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Mina Hashimoto") confirming mina.hashimoto@moveops.com as the correct sender for outbound communications.
OE 17: Update the Terraform Digital Q2 deal in CRM using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "NOTE", company_ids: ["company-terraform-digital"], title referencing Terraform Monday status update or relocation status, body: capturing where each relocation stands -- Priya Dasgupta (Austin to Chicago, May 5, pet-friendly housing needed, no coordinator assigned yet, no apartment search started, no flight booked), Marcus Liu (Portland to NYC, May 12, specialty packing needed for home studio, no coordinator assigned), Samantha Obi (Denver to SF, May 8, minimal furniture plus tech equipment, no coordinator assigned) -- and flagging that the 48-hour proposal deadline from David has likely passed, that coordinators are unassigned, and that no operational work has begun on any of the three moves).
OE 18: Create a Linear ticket for the Terraform Digital Q2 Batch 1 proposal using linear_create_issue (team: the applicable MoveOps team, title referencing Terraform Digital Q2 Batch 1 proposal or coordination, description: covering the 3 relocations with move dates May 5/8/12, all three moves currently unassigned with no coordinators, no housing searches, no vendor coordination, no flights booked; referencing Rachel Nguyen's expectations and the proposal deadline; priority: Urgent or High).
OE 19: Send an email to Rachel Nguyen using send_email (sender: "mina.hashimoto@moveops.com", recipients: ["rachel.nguyen@terraformdigital.com"]) with a status update on the three employees. Content must: acknowledge Mina as the dedicated AM for Terraform, provide a status update showing things are moving for Priya Dasgupta (Austin to Chicago, May 5), Marcus Liu (Portland to NYC, May 12), and Samantha Obi (Denver to SF, May 8), include the pricing framework for the first batch aligned with the internal agreement (honoring the 8% tiered discount for 11-20 moves but not proactively offering the 12% tier -- the first batch of 3 is at standard rates of $2,500/move, with volume tiers activating as they scale), and convey confidence and forward momentum without overpromising on specifics that haven't been confirmed yet.
OE 20: Update the Canopy Health Dr. Mehta deal in CRM using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "NOTE", company_ids: ["company_canopy"], title referencing Dr. Mehta relocation status or Canopy Monday update, body: capturing the latest workstream statuses -- temporary housing: three options presented to Dr. Mehta (River North $1,250/wk, Streeterville $1,350/wk, Gold Coast $1,450/wk) with selection deadline of end of day Friday April 25 and no confirmation yet; equipment packing: inventory received, specialty crating planned (likely via Swift) but not yet formally engaged, chain-of-custody documentation template still needed from Patricia/Canopy compliance (blocker); household move: planned via Swift Relocations for Boston to Chicago but not yet engaged because housing must be confirmed first to determine the delivery address, Dr. Mehta flexible on April 28-30 pack date with delivery targeting May 2-3; estimate EST-2026-0422 for $12,170 prepared and ready).
OE 21: Post a message in the #customer-engagement channel (C002) using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C002", payload: Monday status update covering both accounts). Content must cover: Terraform Digital -- status of each relocation (Priya/Marcus/Samantha), that coordinators are not yet assigned, that no operational work has started, that the proposal deadline has likely passed, and what Mina needs from the team; Canopy Health / Dr. Mehta -- status of each workstream (housing awaiting selection with holds expiring, equipment inventory received but specialty crating not yet formally engaged and chain-of-custody documentation still pending from Canopy compliance, household move planned via Swift but blocked until housing is confirmed to determine delivery address, Dr. Mehta flexible on April 28-30 for pack date), the risk that temporary housing holds expire if Dr. Mehta doesn't respond, and that this is Canopy's first C-suite placement. The tone should be honest about what's on track and what's at risk, since David reads these closely.

query construction
